(born 27 May 1989) is an English international [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) He reached a career high ranking of world number 11 in February 2026.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

**Sam Tolchard** (born 27 May 1989) is an English international [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) He reached a career high ranking of world number 11 in February 2026.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Profile

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sam_Tolchard&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Profile")]

In 2009, he won the singles and triples silver medals at the [Atlantic Bowls Championships](/wiki/Atlantic_Bowls_Championships "Atlantic Bowls Championships")[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) and the following year in 2010, he won the [Hong Kong International Bowls Classic](/wiki/Hong_Kong_International_Bowls_Classic "Hong Kong International Bowls Classic") pairs title with [Robert Newman](/wiki/Robert_Newman_(bowls) "Robert Newman (bowls)").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

He competed for England in the [men's pairs](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2014_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_pairs "Lawn bowls at the 2014 Commonwealth Games – Men's pairs") at the [2014 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2014_Commonwealth_Games "2014 Commonwealth Games")[[8]](#cite_note-8) where he won a bronze medal.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

He was selected as part of the [English team](/wiki/England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "England at the 2018 Commonwealth Games") for the [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games") on the [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland") in [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland")[[11]](#cite_note-11) where he claimed a bronze medal in the [Fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") with [David Bolt](/wiki/David_Bolt_(bowls) "David Bolt (bowls)"), [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney") and [Louis Ridout](/wiki/Louis_Ridout "Louis Ridout").[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In 2019, he won the triples bronze medal at the Atlantic Bowls Championships[[14]](#cite_note-14) and in 2020, he was selected for the [2020 World Outdoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2020_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2020 World Outdoor Bowls Championship") in Australia but the event was cancelled due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[15]](#cite_note-15) In 2022, he competed in the [men's pairs](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_pairs "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's pairs") and the [men's fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") at the [2022 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2022_Commonwealth_Games "2022 Commonwealth Games").[[16]](#cite_note-16) Partnering [Jamie Walker](/wiki/Jamie_Walker_(bowls) "Jamie Walker (bowls)") he won the pairs silver medal, losing out to Wales in the final and in the fours he also secured a bronze medal.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

In November 2022, he won the gold medal at the [World Singles Champion of Champions](/wiki/World_Singles_Champion_of_Champions "World Singles Champion of Champions") in Wellington, New Zealand.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In 2023, he was selected as part of the team to represent England at the [2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship").[[19]](#cite_note-19) He participated in the [men's singles](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_singles "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship – Men's singles") and the [men's pairs](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_Pairs "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship – Men's Pairs") events.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21) In the singles, he won his group undfeated but then lost to [Iain McLean](/wiki/Iain_McLean "Iain McLean") at the quarter final stage.

In November 2025, he won the singles gold medal at the [2025 Bowls World Cup](/wiki/2025_Bowls_World_Cup "2025 Bowls World Cup"), defeating [Dinesh Kumar](/wiki/Dinesh_Kumar_(bowls) "Dinesh Kumar (bowls)") in the final.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## National Achievements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sam_Tolchard&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: National Achievements")]

He has won nine [National Championship](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_Men%27s_National_Championships "List of Bowls England Men's National Championships") titles, including six singles titles.[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24) He bowls outdoors for Kings BC, who have won the [Top Club championship](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Top_Club) "Bowls England National Championships (Top Club)") four years running from 2016 to 2019.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

**National titles**

* 2007 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Champion of Champions)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Champion_of_Champions) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Champion of Champions)")
* 2008 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Four Wood)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Singles_Four_Wood) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Four Wood)")
* 2016 [Bowls England National Championships (men's pairs)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(men%27s_pairs) "Bowls England National Championships (men's pairs)")
* 2016 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Two Wood)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Singles_Two_Wood) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Two Wood)")
* 2019 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Champion of Champions)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Champion_of_Champions) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Champion of Champions)")
* 2021 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Four Wood)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Singles_Four_Wood) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Four Wood)")
* 2021 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Two Wood)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Singles_Two_Wood) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Two Wood)")
* 2022 [Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Two Wood)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Singles_Two_Wood) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Two Wood)")
* 2025 [Bowls England National Championships (men's pairs)](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(men%27s_pairs) "Bowls England National Championships (men's pairs)")

## Personal life and family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sam_Tolchard&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life and family")]

Tolchard is the brother of England international bowler [Sophie Tolchard](/wiki/Sophie_Tolchard "Sophie Tolchard").[[26]](#cite_note-26) His father, [Ray Tolchard](/wiki/Ray_Tolchard "Ray Tolchard"), played cricket for [Devon](/wiki/Devon_County_Cricket_Club "Devon County Cricket Club") and was a [first-class cricket](/wiki/First-class_cricket "First-class cricket") umpire, and his uncle [Roger Tolchard](/wiki/Roger_Tolchard "Roger Tolchard") played [Test cricket](/wiki/Test_cricket "Test cricket") for England and county cricket for [Leicestershire](/wiki/Leicestershire_County_Cricket_Club "Leicestershire County Cricket Club"). Another uncle, [Jeffrey Tolchard](/wiki/Jeffrey_Tolchard "Jeffrey Tolchard"), also played cricket for Leicestershire and played football for [Torquay United](/wiki/Torquay_United_F.C. "Torquay United F.C.") and [Exeter City](/wiki/Exeter_City_F.C. "Exeter City F.C.").[[27]](#cite_note-27)
